key_evidence: P&G announced 7,000 job cuts (~15% of non-manufacturing workforce, ~6% global) over two years, reported as occurring despite increasing profits.
Driver is tariff cost protection ($600M pre-tax headwind) plus $1B-$1.6B restructuring charges, not financial distress.
Event lowers Boundaries (B1 workforce sustainability) and Integrity (I1/I3 consistency under pressure and internal-vs-external consistency); other dimensions unchanged.
Published composite 86.1 reconstructs exactly under the canonical formula, so the change is substantive, not a math-hygiene artifact.

evidence.quote: Procter & Gamble announces plans to layoff 7,000 workers despite increasing profits
evidence.claim: P&G is cutting 7,000 jobs while remaining profitable

evidence.quote: The company had about 108,000 employees as of June 30, 2024, and said the job cuts would account for roughly 15% of its non-manufacturing workforce.
evidence.claim: The cuts represent about 15% of non-manufacturing staff

evidence.quote: Procter & Gamble to cut 7,000 jobs as part of broader restructuring
